title: Forest and treeline comparison richness phase
summary: Fifty-seventh structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-2 wave deepens the forest-to-treeline middle through richer comparison-ready content, then adds one quiet payoff through existing memory or journal-facing seams.

execution_order.why_now: The middle of the biome chain is the best place to reinforce the ecotone identity through content.
A stronger middle-habitat contrast will make the whole world feel more intentional.
The pass should deepen the middle without content sprawl.
Once the content lands, one quiet payoff can make the middle habitats more memorable.
A small payoff can reinforce the ecotone idea without another shell.
The payoff should stay compact and content-owned.

guardrails: Do not reopen station, route-board, or progression systems.
Keep the work in content, notes, comparisons, close looks, or sketchbook memory.
Prefer habitat-role contrast over raw content totals.
Use one source-backed bridge entry plus local note contrast instead of forcing a broad forest-to-treeline shared set.
acceptance_focus: Forest and treeline feel more intentionally contrasted.
The middle of the biome chain reinforces the ecotone identity.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-2.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-forest-and-treeline-comparison-richness-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 2's next long-push wave after the alpine and coastal richness passes: deepen the forest-to-treeline middle through comparison-ready content, then add one compact memory payoff without touching station or progression systems.
